About James

The attorney who
knows both sides
of the courtroom door.

James White III's story didn't start in a law school. It started somewhere most lawyers have never been — and that's exactly what makes him different.

His Journey

From Prisoner to Prosecutor.

James White III's path to the law was not linear. After facing incarceration, he made a deliberate choice to pursue education as the vehicle for change, earning his Bachelor of Arts from Benedict College and his Juris Doctor from The Ohio State University Moritz College of Law.

What makes his law school years remarkable isn't just the degree: it's what he did while pursuing it: mentoring students, speaking inside prisons and juvenile detention centers, and personally assisting individuals with restoration of rights, record restrictions, and pardon applications.

He went on to handle arraignments, motions, bench trials, and jury trials as an Assistant Solicitor General in Fulton County. Today he chairs the BASICS Committee at the State Bar of Georgia, leads the James For Change Foundation as Executive Director, and continues to speak, advocate, and practice law.

Motivated by a passion for justice and equity, James continues to bridge transactional law and public service, using his legal training to advance second chances and promote long-term community stability through education and access.

"Prevention is the highest form of public safety."
Moments Along the Way
With the late Rep. John Lewis
With Sen. Tim Scott
With District Attorney John Herbert “Herb” Cranford Jr. who removed the felony from his record
With former Chief Justice Harold Melton
With U.S. Senator James Lankford as his law clerk on Capitol Hill
With Senator John Ossoff on the campaign trail as Atlanta City Council Candidate

Education

During law school, actively engaged in community outreach: mentoring students, speaking in prisons and juvenile detention centers, and assisting individuals with restoration of rights, record restrictions, and pardon applications.

Bachelor of Arts, Interdisciplinary Studies
Benedict College · Class of 2015
Education

Named Most Outstanding Senior of his graduating class, James served as Student Body President and mentored fellow students at Benedict, forming the roots of a commitment to service that would define his legal career.

The Foundation

James For Change Foundation

Dedicated to reducing recidivism and promoting long-term community stability through legal education, mentorship, and direct advocacy, connecting those the system has left behind to resources, rights, and real second chances.

See Our Programs

Advancing criminal justice reform, educational opportunity, and second chances through law, advocacy, and lived experience.
